perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "Control" means, with respect to any Person, the possession, directly or indirectly, of the affirmative power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of securities, partnership interests or other ownership interests, by contract, by membership or involvement in the board of directors or other management structure of such Person, or otherwise. For purposes hereof, the holding, directly or indirectly, of 30% or more of the issued share capital of a Person shall be deemed the Control of such Person.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 "Control" means, with respect to any Person, the possession, directly or indirectly, of the affirmative power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of securities, partnership interests or other ownership interests, by contract, by membership or involvement in the board of directors or other management structure of such Person, or otherwise. For purposes hereof, the holding, directly or indirectly, of 50% or more of the issued share capital of a Person shall be deemed the Control of such Person.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the percentage of share capital required for 'Control' from 30% to 50%, this creates a contradiction with Section 1.2, which defines Affiliate partly based on 30% ownership. Now, a company could be an Affiliate but not considered to be under the 'Control' of another entity as defined here, leading to inconsistent application of the agreement terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 "Affiliate" means in relation to any Person, any Subsidiary or Holding Company, and any company in which such Person or any such Holding Company holds or Controls directly or indirectly not less than 30% of the issued share capital.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Control' by increasing the percentage of share capital required from 30% to 50%. This directly contradicts Section 1.2, which defines 'Affiliate' based on 30% ownership. A company can now be an Affiliate (due to the 30% threshold in 1.2) without being under the 'Control' of another entity (requiring 50% in the modified 1.12), creating a clear inconsistency and ambiguity in applying the agreement terms. This meets the definition of an Ambiguity creating an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3.1 Within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, furnish to Skype complete and accurate statements (each, a "Statement") in a form acceptable to Skype, certified to be accurate by an officer of the Company or Online BVI, as applicable, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by the Company or Online BVI, or if applicable, any Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3.1 Within sixty (60) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, furnish to Skype complete and accurate statements (each, a "Statement") in a form acceptable to Skype, certified to be accurate by an officer of the Company or Online BVI, as applicable, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by the Company or Online BVI, or if applicable, any Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the statement furnishing period from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' after each quarter creates an inconsistency with Section 5.3.1, which refers to providing payment alongside the Statement. This means the payment deadline could be inconsistently interpreted, as Section 5.3.1 states best efforts to provide payments with the statement, however changing the period of the statement may delay payments on the other clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3.1 Within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, furnish to Skype complete and accurate statements (each, a "Statement") in a form acceptable to Skype, certified to be accurate by an officer of the Company or Online BVI, as applicable, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by the Company or Online BVI, or if applicable, any Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. In the event that Skype provides any Payment Services hereunder, within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, Skype shall furnish to the Company complete and accurate Statements in a form acceptable to Online BVI, certified to be accurate by an officer of Skype, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by Skype, or if applicable, its Subsidiaries or other Affiliates (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Skype Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. The respective Parties shall use their best efforts to accompany each respective Statement with payment, to Skype, if provided by the Company or Online BVI (or their associated third parties set forth above), or to the Company (or Online BVI, as applicable), if provided by Skype (or its associated third parties set forth above), as applicable, of 50% of the Adjusted Net Revenue shown on the respective Statements. Provided that the respective Parties utilize their best efforts to provide such payments together with the respective Statements, no Party shall be deemed in breach hereof for delivering late payment until the date which is sixty (60) days following the last day of the respective applicable calendar quarter. All payments shall be paid in Euros, and in the event revenue is earned by a Party (or its associated third parties set forth above) in currencies other than in Euros, then such Party shall convert said amounts each month into Euros based upon the exchange rate published by the Wall Street Journal as of the fifteenth day of such month or if such day shall fall on a non-business day then as of the first business day following said fifteenth day.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' for furnishing statements directly contradicts the existing text in section 5.3.1, where both the original and contradicted text specify 'thirty (30) days'. This creates a direct conflict regarding the timeframe for providing statements and potentially impacting payment deadlines, which relies on the statement delivery. This inconsistency introduces ambiguity, making the contract potentially unenforceable because of the conflicting deadlines within the same paragraph.

perturbation.original_text: 13.6 Settlement. The Indemnified Party agrees that the Indemnifying Party shall have sole and exclusive control over the defense and settlement of any such third party claim. However, the Indemnifying Party shall not acquiesce to any judgment or enter into any settlement that adversely affects the Indemnified Party's rights or interests without prior written consent of the Indemnified Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.6 Settlement. The Indemnified Party agrees that the Indemnifying Party shall have sole and exclusive control over the defense and settlement of any such third party claim. The Indemnifying Party may acquiesce to any judgment or enter into any settlement, even if it adversely affects the Indemnified Party's rights or interests, without prior written consent of the Indemnified Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for the Indemnifying Party to obtain prior written consent from the Indemnified Party before settling a claim, this directly contradicts the previous sentence and creates uncertainty regarding the extent of the Indemnifying Party's authority and the Indemnified Party's protection. It becomes unclear how the Indemnified Party's rights are to be protected in settlements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.6 Settlement. The Indemnified Party agrees that the Indemnifying Party shall have sole and exclusive control over the defense and settlement of any such third party claim. However, the Indemnifying Party shall not acquiesce to any judgment or enter into any settlement that adversely affects the Indemnified Party's rights or interests without prior written consent of the Indemnified Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Indemnifying Party to settle claims even if it adversely affects the Indemnified Party's rights without consent, directly contradicting the original text's requirement for prior written consent. This creates a clear and significant conflict, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as it makes the extent of the Indemnifying Party's authority and the Indemnified Party's protection uncertain, potentially leading to disputes and unenforceability. The changed text removes a constraint stated in the original version of the text from the same section, creating an omission that directly causes in-text contradiction with the original requirement.
